Event: Pete has his secretary write a fake 6 a.m. Coca-Cola meeting to trick Roger
Explanation: Clever ploy boosts his accounts and status while wasting Roger’s time and undermining him.

Event: Roger reads Pete’s secretary’s calendar to poach accounts
Explanation: Roger gains business at Pete’s direct professional expense.

Event: Pete repeatedly cheats on Trudy
Explanation: He gets short-term pleasure but damages his marriage and hurts her.

Event: Pete forces himself on the German au pair
Explanation: He harms her and later suffers repercussions with little lasting benefit.

Event: Pete tries to impress GM executives in Detroit without learning to drive properly
Explanation: His poor driving preparation hurts his own prospects more than anyone else.

Event: Pete is largely absent from Tammy’s early life
Explanation: His neglect harms his relationship with his daughter without offering clear benefits.

Event: Pete later becomes a loving, doting father to Tammy
Explanation: Improved involvement benefits him emotionally and greatly benefits Tammy.

Event: Pete and Trudy divorce
Explanation: Separating harms both more than it helps, given they later reconcile.

Event: Pete and Trudy reconcile and dissolve their divorce
Explanation: Reconciliation restores mutual support and family stability.

Event: Pete marries into money without realizing his family’s decline
Explanation: He gains stability and connection while they gain a socially suitable match, despite later tensions.

Event: Pete resents and resists financial help from his father-in-law
Explanation: His pride blocks useful support while the in-laws remain relatively unaffected.

Event: Pete asks Trudy to seduce an ex-boyfriend to get his story published
Explanation: He seeks personal advancement at moral and emotional risk to both, with limited payoff.

Event: Trudy refuses to actually commit adultery for Pete’s scheme
Explanation: She protects her integrity and still secures him a lesser publication.

Event: Pete cheats with Peggy
Explanation: He gains gratification and ego boost but harms her and his marriage.

Event: Pete cheats with Beth
Explanation: Affair gratifies him but damages her situation and his own emotional stability.

Event: Pete punches the headmaster over Tammy’s rejection and the clan grudge
Explanation: Violence jeopardizes his reputation while only venting anger and slightly intimidating the headmaster.

Event: Pete confronts Roger over Japanophobia and supports the Honda executives
Explanation: Calling out prejudice helps his professional standing with Honda and pushes the firm toward fairness.

Event: Pete verbally eviscerates Roger over his prejudice on the Honda account
Explanation: He gains authority and strengthens the account while pressuring Roger to moderate his bias.

Event: Pete covers for Don during the security probe about Don’s identity
Explanation: He earns Don’s trust and protects the agency while shielding Don from exposure.

Event: Pete starts demanding more respect from the partners in Season 5
Explanation: Self-assertion improves his status but increases tension with others.

Event: Pete retaliates when Roger poaches his clients
Explanation: Protecting his book of business helps him while undercutting Roger’s advantage.

Event: Pete is a strong internal supporter of the Civil Rights Movement
Explanation: His stance aligns him with social change and benefits marginalized groups.

Event: Pete lashes out at Harry for seeing MLK’s death mainly as a ratings issue
Explanation: His anger risks friction but morally pressures Harry to reconsider a callous attitude.

Event: Pete calls out Roger’s Japanophobia toward Honda executives
Explanation: He improves his rapport with Honda and challenges harmful bigotry.

Event: Pete has Joan prostitute herself for the Jaguar deal
Explanation: He secures a lucrative account at huge moral and personal cost to Joan.

Event: Pete acts disgusted when it appears Trudy dated during their separation
Explanation: His hypocrisy strains relations and offers him no real gain.

Event: Pete confesses love to Peggy and says he should have been with her
Explanation: Unreciprocated confession embarrasses him and emotionally burdens her.

Event: Peggy reveals she had his child and chose not to guilt him
Explanation: Her restraint spared him but confessing now hurts them both more than it helps.

Event: Pete confesses love to Trudy in Season 7B and proposes a new life together
Explanation: His sincere admission rekindles their marriage and secures a happier future.

Event: Pete supports Peggy’s career and predicts she will be Creative Director by 1980
Explanation: Encouragement boosts her confidence and reflects well on his judgment and growth.

Event: Pete helps fix the au pair’s stained dress and returns it
Explanation: He invests time to assist her and gains some goodwill, despite ulterior motives.

Event: Pete visits Tammy during the divorce process
Explanation: His effort to see her aids their bond and offers him emotional connection.

Event: Pete becomes more protective and loving with Trudy in Season 7B
Explanation: His changed behavior strengthens their relationship and mutual well-being.

Event: Pete becomes more supportive of Peggy and Joan in Season 7B
Explanation: Professional and emotional support helps them and reflects his personal growth.

Event: Pete relaxes and lets go of his competition with Don
Explanation: Releasing rivalry improves his mental state and turns him into a valuable ally for Don.

Event: Pete pursues “red-blooded American male” ideals by copying Don
Explanation: Chasing a hollow model leaves him miserable with little real benefit.

Event: Pete makes women he cheats with also cheat on their partners
Explanation: His affairs benefit him at the emotional expense of others’ relationships.

Event: Pete seeks Don’s approval but does not accept Don’s good advice
Explanation: His refusal to learn keeps him stuck while Don’s position remains largely unchanged.
